sourceMovie.title: An American Werewolf in London
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto An American Werewolf in London (1981) as a comedy theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The comedy and horror blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Zombieland
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If An American Werewolf in London clicked for you, Zombieland (2009) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and horror, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after An American Werewolf in London?
faq.answer: Try Zombieland, Shaun of the Dead, What We Do in the Shadows—each shares DNA with An American Werewolf in London while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is An American Werewolf in London?
faq.answer: An American Werewolf in London (1981) is best described as Comedy, Horror. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is An American Werewolf in London part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "An American Werewolf Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
